data.content:
The build generates `GeneratedInfo.cs` successfully but the compiler never sees it. The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes the file but doesn't add it to the `Compile` item group, so Roslyn never compiles it.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `<Compile>` inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<!-- Add the generated file to the compilation so the compiler picks it up -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The MSBuild `Compile` items are collected before the target runs, so the generated file is never passed to the compiler.

The fix is to add a `<Compile Include="..." />` item inside the target after the file is generated, so that MSBuild knows to include it in compilation.

Specifically, I need to add:
```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />


</ItemGroup>
```

after the `WriteCodeFile` task call.

arguments.new_str:
<!-- Add the generated file to the compilation so the compiler picks it up -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

data.content:
Build succeeds and runs correctly now.

**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the `.cs` file to disk but never added it to the MSBuild `<Compile>` item group. By the time `CoreCompile` ran, the compiler didn't know about the generated file.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation.
